perturbation.original_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. Nantz Communications and Nantz acknowledge that the Services to be rendered by Nantz Communications and Nantz are unique and personal. Accordingly, except as otherwise expressly provided below, neither Nantz Communications nor Nantz shall assign any of their respective rights or delegate any of their respective duties or obligations under this Agreement without the written consent of the Company. Nothing herein shall prevent Nantz Communications from assigning the monetary benefits of this Agreement as it may so desire. Further, inasmuch as it is recognized that Nantz Communications is the representative of Nantz, Nantz Communications may at any time assign this Agreement to Nantz and, in such event, Nantz Communications shall have no further obligation or liability in connection herewith and Nantz Communications' position vis-a'-vis the Company in connection herewith shall be in all respects the same as if Nantz Communications had signed this Agreement as agent rather than as a principal from the beginning. The rights granted the Company hereunder shall be used only by it and shall not, without the prior written consent of Nantz Communications or Nantz, be transferred or assigned to any other. In the event of the merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity, Nantz Communications shall have the right to terminate the Agreement by so notifying the Company in writing on or before sixty (60) days Ashworth/Nantz Promotion Agreement - ------------------- after Nantz Communications has received notice of such merger or consolidation if and only if, by virtue of such merger or consolidation Nantz Communications or Nantz would be in default under or violating any provisions of any agreement to which he or it is subject entered into prior to June 1, 1994.
perturbation.changed_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns, regardless of whether such assignment is explicitly permitted. Nantz Communications and Nantz acknowledge that the Services to be rendered by Nantz Communications and Nantz may be delegated to another party at Nantz Communication’s discretion. Accordingly, Nantz Communications nor Nantz may assign any of their respective rights or delegate any of their respective duties or obligations under this Agreement without the written consent of the Company, with Nantz Communication being able to assign benefits to other party. The rights granted the Company hereunder may be used by another entity without prior written consent of Nantz Communications or Nantz. In the event of the merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity, Nantz Communications will have no right to terminate the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This section was designed to make multiple contradiction with in the same section of the document. Starting with the assignment of rights, with the initial text stating that without written consent it can't happen, with the modification we are stating that the assignment is permitted, regardless of whether such assignment is explicitly permitted. We are then contradicting this statement, in the same section when we are saying that without the written consent of the company, neither Nantz Communication, nor Nantz may assign any of their respective rights or delegate any of their respective duties or obligations. Also the rights granted to company, may be used by another entity without prior consent. Also Nantz communication won't have any right to terminate this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns, regardless of whether such assignment is explicitly permitted. Nantz Communications and Nantz acknowledge that the Services to be rendered by Nantz Communications and Nantz may be delegated to another party at Nantz Communication’s discretion. Accordingly, Nantz Communications nor Nantz may assign any of their respective rights or delegate any of their respective duties or obligations under this Agreement without the written consent of the Company, with Nantz Communication being able to assign benefits to other party. The rights granted the Company hereunder may be used by another entity without prior written consent of Nantz Communications or Nantz. In the event of the merger or consolidation of the Company with any other entity, Nantz Communications will have no right to terminate the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions. Initially, it states assignments are permitted regardless of explicit permission, then contradicts itself by requiring company consent for assignments and delegations. Furthermore, it allows the company's rights to be used by another entity without consent, conflicting with the original requirement of prior written consent. Finally, it removes Nantz Communications' right to terminate the agreement in the event of a merger, directly contradicting the original text. All of these create direct, strong contradictions.
